 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.

sglang.title: Refactor SchedulePolicy to improve code organization
sglang.author: libratiger
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

When I try to deep into the  Zero-Overhead Batch Scheduler , I find is hard to get clear on the scheduling, and is hard to impl a new scheduling policy, so I try to refactor SchedulePolicy,and make it easy to add new policy for me and others.
Modifications


Move sorting logic into separ

vllm.title: [Model] Whisper model implementation
vllm.author: aurickq
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add Whisper model implementation. Based on #5964 but heavily optimized and integrated with newer encoder/decoder support in vLLM. Currently only supports audio up to 30s. No VAD model, no beam search, no phoneme model, forced alignment, etc.
Performance overall looks OK, especially at larger batch s

vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

objects that for manage background processes or IPC implements shutdown and weakref.finalize
remove use of __del__ (resolves weird logs when LLM is cleaned up
remove need for higher level classes like LLM, LLMEngine, or AsyncLLM to need to do anything special for shutdown, since all the ob

vllm.title: [V1] Improve TP>1 Error Handling + Stack Trace
vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

handle startup error (VLLM currently hangs if there is an error initializing the model with TP>1)
handle runtime error in LLM when TP>1 (VLLM currently does not clean up all resources in this case)
improve WORKER runtime error stack trace for TP>1 (we currently do not see the root cause)

vllm.title: [Core][Bugfix] Use correct device to initialize GPU data during CUDA-graph-capture
vllm.author: yanburman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Until now, tensors and tiles were created over the default device. When trying to capture a CUDA-graph over other different devices, the code failed since the data was created on a different GPU.
This PR fixes this problem by initializing the data on the correct device.
